rulings.Case Title: EUGENE MILLARD VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 2, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Campbell Soup Company¿s motion for summary judgment is continued on the court¿s own motion to May 7, 2012 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 503. The parties must appear at the May 7 trial call to discuss supplemental briefing dates and a continued hearing date. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: DONALD HAMMON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 2, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Slakey Brothers, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Mr. Haley¿s deposition testimony and declaration and Mr. Ay¿s declaration create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (asbestos cement pipe) attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to the declarations of Mr. Haley and Mr. Ay are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
